title: Quiz: Trading Risk Management
description: Test your understanding of trading risk management techniques

questions.question: What is the primary goal of position sizing?
questions.explanation: Position sizing balances return maximization with risk control. Proper sizing ensures no single trade can cause catastrophic losses while still capturing profit opportunities.

questions.question: What does the Kelly Criterion optimize?
questions.explanation: The Kelly Criterion calculates the optimal bet size to maximize the long-term geometric growth rate of capital. It balances the probability of winning against the payoff ratio.

questions.question: Why is monitoring maximum drawdown critical?

options.text: Large losses are exponentially harder to recover from

questions.explanation: Drawdown monitoring is critical because recovery from large losses requires disproportionately large gains. A 50% loss needs a 100% gain to break even, making early intervention essential.

questions.question: What does Value at Risk (VaR) measure?

options.text: Expected profit over a time period
Maximum expected loss at a given confidence level

questions.explanation: VaR quantifies the maximum expected loss over a given time horizon at a specified confidence level. For example, 95% daily VaR of $1M means there's a 5% chance of losing more than $1M in one day.

questions.question: Trailing stops automatically lock in profits as the price moves favorably.
questions.explanation: True. Trailing stops follow the price as it moves in your favor, ratcheting up the stop level. This automatically locks in profits while still allowing the position room to run.

questions.question: What advantage does Expected Shortfall (CVaR) have over VaR?

options.text: It captures the average loss in the tail beyond VaR

questions.explanation: Expected Shortfall (Conditional VaR) measures the average loss when losses exceed VaR. It provides information about tail risk that VaR ignores, making it a more complete risk measure.

questions.question: What does the Sortino Ratio measure differently from the Sharpe Ratio?

options.text: It uses a different risk-free rate
It only penalizes downside volatility, not upside
It measures monthly instead of daily returns

questions.explanation: The Sortino Ratio uses downside deviation instead of total standard deviation. This only penalizes negative volatility since upside volatility (large gains) is desirable for investors.

questions.question: Which are benefits of ATR-based stop losses? (Select all that apply)

options.text: They adapt to current market volatility
They give positions appropriate room to breathe
They reduce false stop-outs in volatile markets

questions.explanation: ATR-based stops adapt to volatility, giving trades appropriate room based on market conditions. This reduces premature stop-outs while still protecting against large losses. They cannot guarantee profits.
